title: Fix Leaflet map errors and restore entry creation functionality

requirements.text: Rebuild the Leaflet map integration in WorldMapPage.tsx and ATMapPage.tsx from scratch: remove any CDN-based Leaflet script/CSS loading from index.html and instead install Leaflet as an npm package (leaflet + @types/leaflet), import Leaflet CSS directly in the component files, fix the default marker icon broken asset paths by explicitly setting L.Icon.Default.mergeOptions with the correct icon URLs pointing to the leaflet/dist/images assets, and ensure the map container div has an explicit pixel height so the map renders correctly.

quotes: Errors are occurring with the map and entries cannot be made. Reconfigure and retry.

acceptanceCriteria: WorldMapPage renders an interactive Leaflet map without JavaScript errors in the console
ATMapPage renders an interactive Leaflet map without JavaScript errors in the console
Map pins/markers are visible and use correct marker icons (no broken image icons)
Clicking a pin opens a popup with entry details
No duplicate Leaflet CSS or JS loading conflicts between CDN and npm

requirements.text: Fix the AddEntryPage so that new guestbook entries can be successfully submitted. Ensure the PlaceSearchField geocoding integration and form submission do not throw runtime errors, and that the backend addEntry call completes without failure. Remove any dependency on map state or Leaflet initialization that may be blocking or erroring during form submission.
acceptanceCriteria: A user can fill out the Add Entry form and submit it without errors
The entry appears in the GuestbookFeedPage feed after submission
Place search (current location and favorite AT place) works without throwing errors
Form submission does not depend on Leaflet map state being initialized

requirements.text: Audit and clean up frontend/index.html to remove any Leaflet CDN script or link tags (CSS and JS) that conflict with the npm-installed Leaflet package. Ensure only one source of Leaflet is loaded in the application.
acceptanceCriteria: index.html contains no Leaflet CDN <script> or <link> tags
Leaflet is loaded exclusively via the npm package import
No 'L is not defined' or similar global Leaflet reference errors occur

constraints: Do not modify files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui
Keep all Motoko backend logic in backend/main.mo — do not change the backend schema or data model
nonGoals: Adding new map features or new types of map pins beyond what already exists
Changing the visual design or color theme of the application
Modifying backend guestbook entry storage or retrieval logic
